mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.09% signals an extended easing cycle. Sustained low rates compress long-term discount rates and reduce real yields, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to decline as investors rotate into duration. This mechanism operates continuously in low-rate regimes without requiring additional triggers.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% as of June 2019 indicates near-zero policy rate
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with near-zero rates
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 15 basis points over the 20-day window despite FEDFUNDS remaining below 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 6.7% signals labor market slack. When UNRATE exceeds 6.5%, the Fed maintains accommodative policy, which depresses long-term yields. The mechanism triggers when unemployment crosses above 6.5% and remains active until it falls below 5.5%, creating a regime where Treasury yields compress.
regime_hint: labor market slack with elevated unemployment
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 20 basis points in 60% or more of triggered 20-day windows, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 1,091k units and retail sales (RSAFS) at $431.9B jointly signal demand strength. When both series exceed their 12-month moving averages simultaneously, equity valuations expand and the Fed signals higher future rates, causing 10-year yields to rise. The interaction of sustained demand across housing and consumption creates upward yield pressure.
evidence_refs: fact:Both housing and retail sales above recent averages in mid-2019
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 15 basis points in 55% or more of triggered 20-day windows despite both HOUST and RSAFS remaining elevated, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 101.28 indicates supply-side capacity utilization near trend. A 3-month decline in INDPRO below 100.5 signals demand weakness and deflationary pressure, prompting Fed easing and Treasury yield compression. The mechanism activates when INDPRO falls below its 3-month prior level by more than 0.5 index points.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 25 basis points over 60 trading days in 50% or more of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 136.877M and unemployment rate (UNRATE) at 6.7% jointly indicate labor market tightness is easing. When PAYEMS growth slows to below 100k/month while UNRATE rises above 6.5%, the Fed cuts rates aggressively, causing 10-year yields to fall sharply. The interaction of weakening employment and rising unemployment creates strong downward yield pressure.
evidence_refs: fact:Labor market showing mixed signals in mid-2019
regime_hint: labor market deterioration with Fed easing response
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 20 basis points in 60% or more of triggered 20-day windows, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) at 233.887 is stable and below the Fed's 2% target. When year-over-year CPI growth remains below 2.2%, the Fed maintains or eases policy, keeping long-term yields suppressed. This always-on mechanism reflects the structural low-inflation environment of 2019, where yield compression persists regardless of other signals.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 233.887 in May 2019 implies ~1.8% YoY growth
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 30 basis points over 5-day windows in 55% or more of observations, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) at $15,839.3B (Q1 2019) combined with RSAFS at $431.9B signals demand momentum. When quarterly GDP growth falls below 1.5% annualized while retail sales decline month-over-month, recession risk rises and the 10-year yield falls as investors flee to safety. The interaction of slowing growth and weakening consumption creates strong downward yield pressure.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth < 1.5% annualized AND RSAFS declines MoM
evidence_refs: fact:Q1 2019 GDP growth at 3.1%, but trend weakening
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 35 basis points over 60 trading days in 50% or more of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 101.28 and payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 136.877M jointly measure supply-side capacity. When INDPRO rises above 102.0 while PAYEMS grows above 150k/month, supply constraints tighten and inflation expectations rise, causing the 10-year yield to increase. The interaction of strong production and employment growth signals inflationary pressure.
evidence_refs: fact:Both series near or above trend in mid-2019
regime_hint: tight supply conditions with inflation risk
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 25 basis points over 60 trading days in 55% or more of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window.
